title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
summary: Blue Origin has concluded its investigation into the New Glenn rocket's third flight failure, allowing the vehicle to resume launches. The company is now cleared to proceed with future missions.

title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the first Starship V3 on May 22, achieving most of the test objectives during the suborbital flight.

title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Viasat and SES a $437 million contract to build four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program. This initiative aims to enhance military satellite communications.

title: NASA unveils sweeping reorganization
summary: NASA announced a major reorganization on May 22, merging mission directorates and reshuffling personnel. This marks one of the agency's most significant restructuring efforts in recent years.

title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is developing dual-use space infrastructure, including the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia, to enhance strategic resilience in collaboration with AUKUS partners. Full operational capability for DARC Site 1 is targeted for 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, aiming to match their performance even in urban areas. The company positions Starlink Mobile as a future wireless challenger beyond just remote-area backup solutions.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also announced plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: Rocket Lab wins first GEO satellite production contract from U.S. Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to build and operate two GEO satellites with optical payloads. This marks Rocket Lab's first GEO satellite production contract.

title: Ground equipment problem scrubs Starship launch attempt
summary: SpaceX canceled the first launch attempt of its newest Starship vehicle on May 21 due to a ground equipment issue. The scrub highlights challenges in preparing the massive rocket for its maiden flight.

title: Sixth Varda mission successfully returns
summary: Varda Space Industries completed its sixth reentry mission on May 18, focusing on pharmaceutical research and hypersonic testing. The company continues to advance its space-based manufacturing and testing capabilities.

title: SpaceX just launched Starship V3 — its most powerful megarocket yet — into space for the 1st time in spectacular Flight 12 test (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3, the most powerful rocket ever built, for its first test flight. The event showcased the rocket's advanced technology and significant power.

title: 'Once you try these, you won’t want anything else'— Our binoculars expert rated these  as the best small binoculars they'd ever tested. Now at their best price of the year
summary: A binoculars expert rated the Nikon 12x25 Binoculars as the best small binoculars they'd ever tested, praising their image stabilization and pocketable versatility for stargazing.

title: 'For All Mankind's' Ruby Cruz and Ines Asserson on wearing those 'clunky' cool spacesuits and training with a Green Beret (interview)
summary: Actors Ruby Cruz and Ines Asserson discuss the challenges of wearing realistic spacesuits and training with a Green Beret for the show 'For All Mankind'. They highlight the complexity and weight of actual spacesuits compared to expectations.

title: Starship V3 from space: Satellite snaps amazing photo of SpaceX megarocket on the pad
summary: A satellite captured a photo of SpaceX's first Starship V3 megarocket on the launch pad on May 21, just before its scheduled liftoff. The image showcases the massive rocket ahead of its anticipated launch.

title: Shakeup at JPL? Control over iconic NASA center could change for 1st time in nearly 100 years
summary: NASA is considering a major restructuring that could change control over the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) for the first time in nearly a century. This move is part of a broader reorganization effort within the agency.

title: An ancient solar storm left clues in tree rings and a famous poet's diary: 'Red lights in the northern sky'
summary: A study found that an ancient solar storm left traces in tree rings and historical records, including a Japanese poet's diary mentioning 'red lights' in the sky over Kyoto.

title: Cosmic crystal ball reveals an ancient dying star | Space photo of the day for May 22, 2026
summary: A new image captures the Crystal Ball Nebula, the remnants of an ancient dying star in the constellation Taurus. This space photo highlights the nebula's stunning crystalline structure.

title: NASA is updating its Artemis moon base plan. You can find out how on May 26.
summary: NASA is updating its Artemis moon base plan and will announce the details on May 26 during a live broadcast.

title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
summary: NASA is seeking creative partners to help tell the stories of its missions, including Artemis Moon missions, nuclear propulsion, and aeronautics. The agency issued a call for proposals on May 21 for filmmakers, songwriters, and other storytellers to collaborate on mission storytelling.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
summary: NASA will broadcast a live spacewalk by two Roscosmos cosmonauts outside the International Space Station on May 27, starting at 10:15 a.m. EDT and lasting five hours. Coverage will be available on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA's YouTube channel beginning at 9:45 a.m.

title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope released a near-infrared image of a spiral arm of Messier 51 (M51) as part of a study of nearly 9,000 star clusters. The data reveals that more massive star clusters form more frequently.

title: Keeping NASA Flying: Ground Crews Ensure Aircraft Readiness
summary: NASA relies on ground crews at Armstrong Flight Research Center to maintain aircraft readiness for research and science missions. The center has recently added new aircraft to its fleet.

title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
summary: NASA plans to compete the next contract for managing the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), currently operated by Caltech since its inception. The move aims to ensure accountability and maximize value for U.S. taxpayers.

title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
summary: NASA announced an agencywide realignment to accelerate mission delivery and align with the National Space Policy. The changes aim to improve speed and efficiency in achieving national space objectives.

title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
summary: A new material discovered at NASA's Glenn Research Center could enable astronauts to melt Moon rocks and extract valuable resources like metals and oxygen for future lunar missions. This innovation supports NASA's goal of utilizing lunar resources to reduce payload weight and sustain long-term exploration.

title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy cluster with galaxies of various shapes and sizes, along with foreground stars exhibiting diffraction spikes.

title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA will showcase a mission to boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using Northrop Grumman's Pegasus XL rocket and Katalyst's LINK robotic spacecraft. The event is scheduled for June 17 at NASA's Wallops Flight Facility in Virginia.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3 upgrades, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, demonstrating impressive performance during its maiden flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key advancement for future space missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key testing for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. This follows multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab signed new launch contracts and acquired a robotics company in Q1 2026, surpassing all its 2025 records. The company continued its growth trajectory with these strategic moves.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket, preparing for its first launch. The new infrastructure marks a significant step in the company's efforts to enhance its launch capabilities.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt, following a previous docking by Progress MS-34. The mission marks another resupply effort to the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China are preparing for nine launches in the week of May 11, marking a busy period for space missions. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section test materials to ensure they survive space conditions, supported by young innovators. This effort helps guarantee the success of ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses the importance of sensitivity and sturdiness in space industry components. It highlights advancements in materials and technology to meet these requirements.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission is undergoing a critical parachute deployment test to ensure safe landing. The bake-out process helps remove moisture and contaminants from the parachute fabric.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites. This innovation could significantly enhance space debris removal and satellite servicing operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed critical space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that existing military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities in the country.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double its funding for the space sector to accelerate growth. Critics argue current investments are insufficient despite recent increases.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey identified improved lunar technology as the top priority for space tech investments. The survey aimed to address key technical challenges in space exploration.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research affordably. This initiative demonstrates ESA's commitment to cost-effective scientific exploration.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO. The company's financial data was revealed in preparation for the public offering expected next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could accelerate the nuclear arms race. The system is intended to protect America but may have the opposite effect.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve collaborations with Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over sensitive technology sharing.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in engineering production. The company is shifting from traditional design reviews to a more advanced 'decision intelligence' approach.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000 depending on the scenario, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-volume, high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company's ground technology advancements amid a dynamic market. The move highlights the company's focus on cloud-native and interoperable solutions in the satellite communications sector.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness (SDA) software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the UK government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build the first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness mission. This marks Rocket Lab's entry into GEO satellite manufacturing.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones using Viasat's L-band technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its development and deployment efforts through this investment.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force's Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and develop innovative solutions for maintaining satellites in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This technology aims to simplify access to and analysis of satellite imagery and data.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the first public glimpse into its financials. This marks a significant step for the company as it prepares to go public.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) raises concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence on critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to heavily impact the growing space sector.
